title: How to complete your return - Canada.ca
content:
Electing under section 217

## How to complete your return
Follow the steps below to report you eligible section 217 income using the return and schedules.

## Steps to complete your return
- Get instructions to complete your section 217 return For instructions on how to complete your section 217 return, refer to Income Tax and Benefit Guide for Non-residents and Deemed Residents of Canada . If you left Canada permanently Use the tax package for the province or territory where you resided on the date you left Canada.

### Get instructions to complete your section 217 return
For instructions on how to complete your section 217 return, refer to Income Tax and Benefit Guide for Non-residents and Deemed Residents of Canada .
Use the tax package for the province or territory where you resided on the date you left Canada.
- Gather all of your documents You will need the following documents to complete your return: Information slips (such as NR4 slips ) Receipts for any deductions or credits you want to claim Forms and schedules from the Income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada

### Gather all of your documents
You will need the following documents to complete your return:
- Information slips (such as NR4 slips )
- Receipts for any deductions or credits you want to claim
- Forms and schedules from the Income tax package for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada
- Indicate that you are making a section 217 election Write “SECTION 217” at the top of page 1 of your Income Tax and Benefit Return for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada .

### Indicate that you are making a section 217 election
Write “SECTION 217” at the top of page 1 of your Income Tax and Benefit Return for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada .
- Complete the Identification and other information section Get detailed instructions for completing: Identification and other information .

### Complete the Identification and other information section
Get detailed instructions for completing: Identification and other information .
- Report your income Report the following income on your return: All eligible section 217 income paid or credited to you Canadian-source employment and business income Net Canadian partnership income (if you are a limited or non-active partner) Any taxable capital gains from disposing of taxable Canadian property Special rules for part-year residents As a part-year resident electing to file a return under section 217 , you must report: Your world income for the part of the year that you were a resident of Canada All section 217 income for the part of the year that you were a non-resident of Canada For more information about reporting world income, see the Income Tax and Benefit Guide for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada . Part-year residents electing under section 217 pay provincial or territorial tax instead of the surtax for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada. For more information, see Individuals – Leaving or entering Canada and non-residents .
Report the following income on your return:
- All eligible section 217 income paid or credited to you
- Canadian-source employment and business income
- Net Canadian partnership income (if you are a limited or non-active partner)
- Any taxable capital gains from disposing of taxable Canadian property
As a part-year resident electing to file a return under section 217 , you must report:
- Your world income for the part of the year that you were a resident of Canada
- All section 217 income for the part of the year that you were a non-resident of Canada
For more information about reporting world income, see the Income Tax and Benefit Guide for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada .
Part-year residents electing under section 217 pay provincial or territorial tax instead of the surtax for non-residents and deemed residents of Canada. For more information, see Individuals – Leaving or entering Canada and non-residents .
- Complete your schedules and calculate your federal tax Complete each schedule in the following order if it applies to you: Schedule C , Electing under Section 217 of the Income T ax Act – Part 1 and Part 2 Schedule A, Statement of World Income Income Tax and Benefit Return for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada – Step 5 Schedule B, Allowable amount of federal non-refundable tax credits

### Complete your schedules and calculate your federal tax
Complete each schedule in the following order if it applies to you:
- Schedule C , Electing under Section 217 of the Income T ax Act – Part 1 and Part 2
- Schedule A, Statement of World Income
- Income Tax and Benefit Return for Non-Residents and Deemed Residents of Canada – Step 5
- Schedule B, Allowable amount of federal non-refundable tax credits
- Amounts withheld from your information slips Enter the non-resident tax withheld on your eligible section 217 income from your NR4 slips on line 43700 of your return.

### Amounts withheld from your information slips
Enter the non-resident tax withheld on your eligible section 217 income from your NR4 slips on line 43700 of your return.
- Send your supporting documents and completed return to the CRA Attach your information slips and completed schedules to your return. If you file your return without attaching your schedules and supporting documents, the assessment of your return may be delayed. Send your documents to one of the tax centres below: You were a non-resident of Canada and lived in a different country in the year Send your section 217 return and supporting documents to the address shown below for the country you lived in. If you lived in: Denmark France the Netherlands the United Kingdom the United States Mail to: Winnipeg Tax Centre PO Box 14001, Station Main Winnipeg MB R3C 3M3 For all other countries Sudbury Tax Centre 1050 Notre Dame Avenue Sudbury ON P3A 5C2 You are a newcomer to Canada Send your section 217 return and supporting documents to the address shown below for the province or territory you lived in on December 31 , 2025. If you lived in: Alberta British Columbia Manitoba Northwest Territories Nunavut Saskatchewan Yukon Ontario (Belleville, Hamilton, Kingston, Kitchener, London, Ottawa, Peterborough, St. Catharines , Thunder Bay , Waterloo, Windsor) Mail to: Winnipeg Tax Centre PO Box 14001, Station Main Winnipeg MB R3C 3M3 New Brunswick Newfoundland and Labrador Nova Scotia Prince Edward Island Québec Ontario (Barrie, Sudbury, Toronto) Sudbury Tax Centre 1050 Notre Dame Avenue Sudbury ON P3A 5C2

### Send your supporting documents and completed return to the CRA
Attach your information slips and completed schedules to your return.
If you file your return without attaching your schedules and supporting documents, the assessment of your return may be delayed.
Send your documents to one of the tax centres below:
Send your section 217 return and supporting documents to the address shown below for the country you lived in.
PO Box 14001, Station Main Winnipeg MB R3C 3M3
For all other countries
1050 Notre Dame Avenue Sudbury ON P3A 5C2
Send your section 217 return and supporting documents to the address shown below for the province or territory you lived in on December 31 , 2025.
- Northwest Territories
Ontario (Belleville, Hamilton, Kingston, Kitchener, London, Ottawa, Peterborough, St. Catharines , Thunder Bay , Waterloo, Windsor)
PO Box 14001, Station Main Winnipeg MB R3C 3M3
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Prince Edward Island
Ontario (Barrie, Sudbury, Toronto)
1050 Notre Dame Avenue Sudbury ON P3A 5C2
